income_thresholds.description: Annual family income must be below Rs 3 lakh; BPL families with priority ration cards automatically eligible. Covers the lowest 40% families (~42 lakh families in Kerala).
income_thresholds.source: Kerala KASP Guidelines / Kerala Government Order

exclusion_rules.description: Any family member paying income tax disqualifies the household
Government employees and pensioners covered under separate Kerala govt medical schemes
Families with employer-provided health insurance are excluded
Non-priority ration card holders may need additional income verification for eligibility
NO exclusion for pre-existing conditions; all pre-existing conditions are covered from day 1 with no waiting period

family_criteria.family_definition: All members listed on the ration card are covered. Includes head of family, spouse, children, and dependent parents. No cap on family size. Approximately 42 lakh families (lowest 40%) eligible.

covered_procedures: Pre-existing conditions covered from day 1 (no waiting period)
Cardiology (bypass surgery, angioplasty, valve replacement, pacemaker)
Orthopedic (knee replacement, hip replacement, spinal surgery)
Neurosurgery (brain surgery, spinal cord procedures, craniotomy)
Burns treatment and reconstructive plastic surgery
Diagnostics and lab tests during hospitalization

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Required for Aadhaar-based e-KYC verification at empanelled hospital or Akshaya centre

required_documents.name: Ration Card (Kerala)
alternatives: BPL Certificate from Kerala Revenue Department
required_documents.notes: Priority (pink) or AAY (yellow) ration card required; primary eligibility proof

required_documents.name: Income Certificate
required_documents.notes: Needed if ration card type alone does not confirm BPL status or income below Rs 3 lakh

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: For OTP verification and scheme notifications

required_documents.name: Referral Letter
required_documents.notes: Referral from government hospital/PHC preferred but not mandatory for emergencies

enrollment_channels: Akshaya centres (Common Service Centres in Kerala)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Check eligibility at Akshaya centre or call Disha helpline 1056 with ration card details
Step 2: Visit nearest KASP-empanelled hospital or Akshaya centre with Aadhaar and ration card
Step 3: Complete Aadhaar-based e-KYC (biometric or OTP verification)
Step 4: Operator verifies ration card and BPL status against Kerala Civil Supplies database
Step 5: KASP beneficiary card generated under integrated AB-PMJAY umbrella linking Aadhaar to ration card family details
Step 6: For treatment, visit empanelled hospital; pre-authorization raised by hospital
Step 7: Cashless treatment provided after approval; pre-existing conditions covered from day 1 with no waiting period
description_for_embedding: Karunya Arogya Suraksha Padhathi (KASP) is Kerala's state health insurance scheme providing Rs 5 lakh (Rs 500000) per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization. It covers the lowest 40% of families in Kerala (~42 lakh families) with annual income below Rs 3 lakh holding BPL/priority ration cards. KASP is integrated under the AB-PMJAY (Ayushman Bharat) umbrella and enrollment is done via Akshaya centres across Kerala. A key benefit is that all pre-existing conditions are covered from day 1 with no waiting period. The scheme covers over 900 medical and surgical procedures at empanelled government and private hospitals. Formerly known as RSBY and CHIS Plus, the scheme was restructured as KASP in 2019. Free cashless treatment for eligible families. Portal: sha.kerala.gov.in.
